task_completion.description: Open the browser's Developer Tools and navigate to the Network tab
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser window is open with the Developer Tools pane visible, and the 'Network' tab is actively selected.

task_completion.description: Enable the 'Preserve log' setting in the Network tab
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Preserve log' checkbox in the Developer Tools Network tab is checked. This is required to capture multiple page navigations in a single trace.

task_completion.description: Navigate to and capture the forms/post endpoint
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows 'https://httpbin.org/forms/post' and/or the Network tab shows a recorded request to 'post' or 'forms/post'.

task_completion.description: Navigate to and capture the delay/3 endpoint
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows 'https://httpbin.org/delay/3' and/or the Network tab shows a recorded request to 'delay/3'.

task_completion.description: Navigate to and capture the status/503 endpoint
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows 'https://httpbin.org/status/503' and/or the Network tab shows a recorded request to 'status/503' with a 503 status code.

task_completion.description: Export the network trace as a HAR file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the 'Export HAR' button (often a downward arrow icon in the Network tab) or right-clicks a request and selects 'Save all as HAR with content'.

task_completion.description: Save the HAR file as 'incident_trace.har' to the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog or file manager window is visible, showing the file being named 'incident_trace.har' and saved in the 'Desktop' directory (/home/ga/Desktop).

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical web browser to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: A standard web browser (like Edge, Chrome, or Firefox) is visible in the screenshots. The agent did not use terminal commands like 'curl' or 'wget' to bypass browser interaction.

integrity.description: The HAR file was exported using the browser's built-in tools
integrity.visual_evidence: The HAR file is generated via the Developer Tools GUI export feature, not by writing a script (e.g., Python) or manually typing JSON into a text editor.

integrity.description: The network tab shows genuine request data
integrity.visual_evidence: The Network tab displays realistic request rows with varying times, sizes, and appropriate status codes (e.g., a 503 status for the status/503 endpoint), indicating actual network interaction.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must capture network traffic from three specific URLs: https://httpbin.org/forms/post, https://httpbin.org/delay/3, and https://httpbin.org/status/503. To capture all three in a single HAR file without losing previous requests upon navigation, 'Preserve log' must be enabled in the browser's Developer Tools Network tab. The final file must be named exactly 'incident_trace.har' and saved to the Desktop (/home/ga/Desktop).
